conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria, and then I'll ask you some questions about your situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit. To get started, I need to ask you some questions about your situation.

Do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a son called Alex. He's 6 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's good to know about Alex!

To continue assessing your eligibility, could you tell me: Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great. Now I need to ask: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or has anyone else claimed it for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you or your partner (if you have one) earning over £60,000 per year individually?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't earn anywhere near that much.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me summarize your situation:
- You have a son named Alex who is 6 years old
- Alex lives at home with you
- You live in the UK (Birmingham)
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- You don't earn over £60,000 per year

Based on the official UK government eligibility criteria, you meet all the requirements for Child Benefit for Alex because:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You are responsible for him (he lives with you)
- You live in the UK
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, and that would be you

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the necessary criteria:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You're responsible for him and he lives with you
- You live in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. If you haven't already claimed, you can do so through the government's official channels.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. He is 6 years old (under 16), lives at home with the claimant in the UK (Birmingham), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. According to UK government rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 6-year-old son Alex. The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex who lives with them, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for the child. All standard eligibility requirements are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. He is 6 years old (under 16), lives at home with the claimant in the UK (Birmingham), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. According to UK government rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 6-year-old son Alex. The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex who lives with them, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for the child. All standard eligibility requirements are satisfied.
